Table of Contents

Interface IForceDirectedLayout

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.Geodatabase
Assembly
ESRI.Server.Geodatabase.dll

Provides access to members that control the Force Directed layout.

[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
[Guid("F9DA15DD-7A8C-4575-92C2-EFAB0CE14926")]
public interface IForceDirectedLayout : INetworkDiagramLayout

Properties

BreakPointPosition

Indicates the relative position of the two inflexion points that are inserted along the diagram edges to compute the curved edges geometry when EdgesType is 2. This parameter is used only when EdgesType is esriLayoutCurvedEdge.

double BreakPointPosition { get; set; }

Property Value

double

Examples

Sample Snippet

DegreeOfFreedom

Indicates the degree of freedom factor that is used to move the diagram junctions during each Force Directed layout iteration.

esriLayoutDegreeOfFreedom DegreeOfFreedom { get; set; }

Property Value

esriLayoutDegreeOfFreedom

Examples

Sample Snippet

EdgesType

Indicates the type of display for the diagram edges (curved or not).

esriLayoutEdgesType EdgesType { get; set; }

Property Value

esriLayoutEdgesType

Examples

Sample Snippet

IsActive 

bool IsActive { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

Name 

string Name { get; }

Property Value

string

NumberOfIterations

Indicates the number of Force Directed layout iterations that is executed before the final result is shown.

int NumberOfIterations { get; set; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

PreserveContainers 

bool PreserveContainers { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

PropertySet 

IPropertySet PropertySet { get; }

Property Value

IPropertySet

RepelFactor

Indicates how much diagram junctions that are very close are spread out. The larger this parameter value, the greater the distance that is added between nearly overlapping diagram junctions.

double RepelFactor { get; set; }

Property Value

double

Examples

Sample Snippet

Version 

int Version { get; }

Property Value

int
